meta_title: For Students: freelance pitching to Solve stuck with basic apps (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to freelance pitching and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: Freelance Pitching to Escape Basic App Loops
content.seo_description: Break out of the basic app loop. Learn how to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to pitch professional freelance projects and build a high-value portfolio.
content.answer_block: Stop building generic to-do lists. Transition to professional growth by identifying real-world business problems and pitching custom AI solutions. By using Antigravity for rapid prototyping and Claude Code for iterative development, you can move from a student developer to a project-ready freelancer who delivers actual business value.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks grinding through tutorials, building yet another weather app or a generic blog. The code works, but you feel the stagnation setting in—that hollow realization that your portfolio is indistinguishable from thousands of others. You are stuck in the 'tutorial purgatory' where the path from code to impact remains invisible.

But the gap between 'student' and 'freelancer' isn't a lack of technical knowledge; it's a lack of commercial application. The shift happens when you stop building for yourself and start building for someone else’s bottom line. Today, we bridge that gap by using high-leverage tools to turn your coding skills into a professional pitch that commands attention and pays dividends.

steps.title: Identify the High-Value Niche
steps.description: Focus on local businesses with manual data entry or communication bottlenecks. Your goal is to find a friction point that an AI agent can automate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze a list of 10 common small business workflows (e.g., HVAC dispatch, dental scheduling) and identify 3 specific areas where an AI-driven automation could save at least 5 hours per week.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Research common workflow bottlenecks in local service-based businesses and output a market-fit summary.'

steps.title: Build the MVP Prototype
steps.description: Use Antigravity to scaffold the core logic. Don't worry about UI polish yet; focus on the data flow and the core AI utility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Python-based backend architecture for a lead-qualification bot that integrates with a CRM API to filter inbound emails.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Create a robust backend structure with error handling and logging for a lead qualification MVP.'

steps.title: Craft the Value-Based Pitch
steps.description: Your pitch should never be about 'code' or 'AI'. It must be about 'saved time' and 'increased revenue'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a 150-word cold email pitch for a local business owner that highlights the cost of their current manual process and proposes a 14-day pilot of the AI tool.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Draft a professional cold outreach template focused on ROI metrics for a small business owner.'

steps.title: Iterative Refinement with Claude Code
steps.description: As you get feedback from the pitch, use Claude Code to rapidly refactor your prototype based on real-world constraints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the existing lead-qualification logic to handle edge-case scenarios where the user input is ambiguous.
steps.claude_code_command: claude fix 'Refactor the email parsing logic to handle complex, non-standard user inputs with higher accuracy.'

steps.title: Deployment and Documentation
steps.description: Package your project as a professional asset. Documentation is what separates a hobbyist from a freelancer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a README.md and a project documentation suite that explains the architecture, security, and maintenance steps for the end-user.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate comprehensive documentation for the project including setup instructions and security best practices.'

pro_tips: Always pitch a '14-day pilot' rather than a long-term contract; it lowers the barrier to entry for hesitant business owners.
Never show code during a pitch; show the output (the report, the email, or the database entry) to keep the focus on value.
Use Claude Code to generate unit tests before you even write the feature; it forces you to define the 'done' state clearly.
When a business says 'no', ask for one specific reason why—that reason is your next product feature.
Build a 'Case Study' page instead of a 'Portfolio' page; clients want to see the problem you solved, not the libraries you used.
content.next_steps: Once your first pilot is live, document the results in a case study and reach out to three similar businesses in the same industry.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to move beyond basic apps and into the world of professional software delivery. Stop waiting for permission to be an engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
